usage_message:
📖 **Available commands:**

```
/help              — Show this command list
/status            — Check agent status
/usage             — Show credit usage report
/doctor            — Run deployment diagnostics
/cancel            — Cancel current task
/interrupt         — Interrupt a stuck task
/cwd               — Open project/CWD setup panel
/reset             — Restart agent session
/start             — Bind a project directory
/pause             — Switch to @mention inline mode
/back              — Resume full-listen thread mode
/thread            — Show or set new-thread behavior
/resume            — Re-display last truncated response
/model             — Show or switch model
/models            — List available models
/agent             — List or switch agent mode
/mcp               — Manage channel MCP policy
/steering          — Manage agent context file
/memory            — Manage persistent memory rules
/flashmemory       — Manage session flash memory
/compact           — Compact conversation context
/clear             — Clear conversation history
```
All commands also work with `!` prefix (e.g. `!help`, `!reset`).
Send any message to chat with the agent.
error.thread_only: ❌ This command can only be used inside a thread.
error.channel_only: ❌ This command must be used in the parent channel.
error.bot_not_in_channel: ❌ This bot is not available in this channel.
error.parse_schedule:
❌ Invalid cron expression: %s
Format: `min hour day month weekday`
Examples: `*/30 * * * *` (every 30 min), `0 9 * * 1-5` (weekdays 9am)
error.not_cron_format:
Not a valid cron expression. Use 5 fields: min hour day month weekday
Examples: `*/30 * * * *`, `0 9 * * *`, `0 9,18 * * 1-5`
error.parse_time_or_empty:
❌ Cannot parse time or content is empty
Usage: `!remind <time> <content>`
Formats: `+30m`, `+2h`, `17:00`, `tomorrow 09:00`
error.parse_time_help: Cannot parse time. Supported: +30m, +2h, 17:00, tomorrow 09:00, 明天 09:00, 30分鐘後
error.cwd_outside_allowlist:
❌ Working directory is outside the allowed roots.
%s
Run `/doctor` to see the configured allowlist, or choose a path under an allowed root.
error.cwd_not_found:
❌ Working directory was not found.
%s
Create the directory first or use `/cwd` to choose an existing project path.
error.cwd_not_directory:
❌ The selected working path is not a directory.
%s
Use `/cwd <directory>` or `/start <directory>` with a project folder.
error.kiro_cli_missing:
❌ kiro-cli cannot be resolved.
%s
Check `KIRO_CLI_PATH`, then run `/doctor` again.
error.kiro_auth:
❌ kiro-cli is not authenticated.
%s
Run `kiro-cli login` on the host or set `KIRO_API_KEY`, then restart the bot.
error.queue_full_action:
❌ The channel queue is full.
%s
Wait for the current task to finish or use `/cancel` before sending more work.
error.no_thread_agent:
❌ No active or saved thread agent is available for this thread.
If this is only a delivery thread from the parent channel, run the command in the parent channel. To create a thread agent here, send a normal message in this thread first.
error.active_job_not_cancellable: ❌ A task is starting but is not cancellable yet. Try `/cancel` again in a moment.
error.thread_agent_limit_all_active:
❌ Cannot start another thread agent right now. Capacity is full (%d max, %d active).
All thread agent slots are currently working. Wait for one to finish, then retry.
error.thread_agent_limit_choose:
❌ Cannot start another thread agent right now. Capacity is full (%d max: %d active, %d inactive).
No agent was closed automatically. Close one inactive thread agent with `/close-thread thread_id:<id>`, then retry this message.
Oldest inactive candidates:
%s
setup.required.command: ❌ This channel must be initialized with `/cwd` before using `%s`.
silent.on: 🔇 Silent mode enabled — compact tool output
silent.off: 🔊 Silent mode disabled — full tool output
thread_mode.on: 🧵 Thread mode enabled — new channel tasks will open Discord threads.
thread_mode.off: 💬 Thread mode disabled — new channel tasks require @mention and reply in-channel with emoji progress.
thread_mode.status.on: 🧵 Thread mode is **on**. New channel tasks open Discord threads.
thread_mode.status.off: 💬 Thread mode is **off**. New channel tasks require @mention and reply in-channel with emoji progress.
reset.success: ✅ Session reset. Next message starts a new agent.
interrupt.success: ⚠️ Interrupt requested; if the current task remains stuck, repeat `/interrupt` to try another process interrupt.
cwd.set: ✅ CWD set to `%s`, takes effect on next reset.
cwd.forbidden: ❌ You need Discord channel management permissions to view or change the working directory.
cwd.setup.default_root: Initial setup is restricted to DEFAULT_CWD project root: `%s`.
cwd.setup.no_projects: No existing projects were found under DEFAULT_CWD. Create a new project to continue.
cwd.setup.no_selectable_projects: Discovered projects could not fit Discord select-menu limits. Create a shorter project name or use `/cwd path` as an advanced fallback.
cwd.setup.project_count: Select one of %d discovered project(s), or create a new one.
cwd.setup.confirm:
Confirm channel project setup?

Project: `%s`
Path: `%s`

The channel working directory will be set only after confirmation.
cwd.setup.modal.title: Create Project
cwd.setup.modal.name: Project name
cwd.setup.initialized: ✅ Channel initialized with project `%s`. Send the next message to start the agent.
cwd.setup.created: ✅ Created and selected project `%s`. Send the next message to start the agent.
cwd.setup.default_mcp_enabled: ✅ Default `bot-tools` MCP is enabled for safe bot output and non-destructive bot utilities.
cwd.setup.default_mcp_failed: ⚠️ Default `bot-tools` MCP could not be enabled: %s
cwd.setup.next_mcp: Next step: review MCP tool access for this channel.
cwd.setup.channel_uninitialized_admin: This channel is not initialized yet. Open setup to select or create a project before the agent can run.
cwd.setup.channel_uninitialized_user: This channel is not initialized yet. Ask a channel manager to select or create a project before using the agent.
cwd.setup.thread_uninitialized_admin: This thread's parent channel is not initialized yet. Open setup to select or create a project before the agent can run.
cwd.setup.thread_uninitialized_user: This thread's parent channel is not initialized yet. Ask a channel manager to select or create a project before using the agent.
usage.report.title: **Usage credits**
usage.report.range: Day from `%s`, week from `%s`, month from `%s`
usage.report.no_records: No usage records in the current month.
usage.report.no_user_records: No usage records for this user in the current month.
usage.report.unmetered: -# %d turn(s) did not include credit metadata from kiro.
status.format: Agent: `%s` | State: `%s` | Queue: %d | Session: `%s` | Model: `%s` | kiro: `%s` | bot: `%s` | Agent uptime: `%s` | Context: `%.1f%%`
doctor.discord.permissions.title: Discord permissions
doctor.bot_peers.title: Bot peers
doctor.bot_peers.no_channel_trigger: ↳ discovered peers, but none can respond in this channel/thread
egress.sensitive_path_notice: Sensitive path detected; uploaded a sanitized copy.
egress.reason.not_text: file type is not safely redactable as text
egress.reason.is_directory: directories cannot be sent as files
egress.reason.extract_failed: failed to extract readable text from this file
egress.reason.unsupported_format: file format is not supported for safe text extraction
cmd.start.desc: Bind project directory and start agent
cmd.usage.opt.user: User to report (leave empty for top users)
cmd.audit.desc: Show recent audit events for this channel or thread
cmd.audit.opt.limit: Number of recent events to show
cmd.mcp.sub.manage: Open the interactive MCP policy panel
cmd.mcp.sub.status: Show the MCP checklist and current channel policy
cmd.mcp.sub.enable: Enable an MCP server for this channel
cmd.mcp.sub.disable: Disable an MCP server for this channel
mcp.forbidden: ❌ You need Discord channel management permissions to change MCP policy.
mcp.usage: Usage: `!mcp status [server]` | `!mcp enable <server>` | `!mcp disable <server>`. Use `/mcp manage` for interactive server/tool management and MCP reload.
mcp.enabled: ✅ MCP server `%s` enabled with full server access. The current agent was stopped if it was active; send the next message to restart it with the new MCP policy.
mcp.disabled: ✅ MCP server `%s` disabled. The current agent was stopped if it was active; send the next message to restart it with the new MCP policy.
mcp.tool_updated: ✅ MCP server `%s` tool policy updated for `%s`. The current agent was stopped if it was active; send the next message to restart it with the new MCP policy.
mcp.restarted: ✅ MCP policy reload requested. Stopped %d active agent(s); next message starts with current policy.
mcp.policy_no_tools: MCP server `%s` would expose no tools with this policy. Use `full` to expose all tools, or use `/mcp manage` to scan and allow specific tools.
mcp.manage.slash_only: Use `/mcp manage` to open the interactive MCP panel.
mcp.manage.summary: Discovered %d MCP server(s). Select a server, then apply a policy.
mcp.manage.truncated: Showing the first %d servers because Discord select menus are limited to 25 options.
mcp.manage.server_detail:
**%s**
State: `%s` | Policy: `%s`
Allow all tools: `%s`
Tools: `%s`
mcp.tools.empty: No cached tools yet. Click `Scan tools` to discover this server's tools.
mcp.tools.summary: %d cached tool(s). Page %d/%d, tools %d-%d. 🟢 allowed: %d · ⚪ blocked: %d. Use the menus below to change this page.
audit.forbidden: ❌ You need Discord channel management permissions to view audit events for this target.
audit.slash_only: ❌ Audit queries can expose operational history and are only available through the private slash command `/audit`.
audit.no_records: No audit records for this target.
audit.prompt_requires_init: ❌ Channel must be initialized before using audit prompt. Use `/cwd` to set up the channel first.
audit.prompt_submitted: 🔍 Audit investigation submitted to the agent. The private response will appear shortly.
cmd.audit.opt.prompt: Natural language question to investigate in the audit database
cmd.cwd.opt.path: Advanced: set an explicit working directory path
cmd.silent.desc: Show or set silent mode (compact tool output)
cmd.silent.opt.mode: on = compact, off = full detail (default: on)
cmd.thread.desc: Show or set thread mode for new channel tasks
cmd.thread.opt.mode: on = open threads, off = in-channel mention-only replies
cmd.model.opt.model: Model ID (leave empty to show current)
cmd.agent.opt.mode: Mode ID (leave empty to list modes)
cmd.cron_run.opt.name: Task name
cmd.cron_prompt.desc: Create a scheduled task using natural language
cmd.cron_prompt.opt: Describe what you want scheduled (e.g. 'every day at 9am check server status')
cron.prompt.failed: ❌ Could not parse your request after %d attempts. Please try rephrasing or use `/cron` for manual setup.
cron.prompt.no_schedule:
❓ Your description doesn't include clear schedule/timing information.
Please include when you want it to run, e.g.:
• 每天早上9點...
• every Monday at 3pm...
• 每月5號...
cron.modal.title: Add Scheduled Task
cron.modal.name: Task Name
cron.modal.prompt_ph: e.g. Check server CPU, memory, disk usage and compare with last run
cron.created:
✅ Scheduled task created
Name: **%s**
Cron: `%s` → %s
Prompt: %s
cron.list.empty: 📋 No scheduled tasks in this channel. Use `/cron` to add one.
remind.usage:
Usage: `!remind <time> <content>`
e.g. `!remind 5pm remind me about the meeting`
cmd.remind.opt.agent: Let agent execute the task (default: just notify)
cmd.close_thread.desc: Close a thread agent by thread ID
cmd.compact.desc: Compress conversation history to free context
thread_agent.not_running: ℹ️ No active thread agent is running in this thread.
thread_agent.not_running_thread: ℹ️ No active thread agent is running for `%s`.
thread_agent.close_thread_forbidden: ❌ That thread agent is outside this channel scope.
thread_agent.close_thread_active: ❌ That thread agent is currently working. Use `/cancel` or `/interrupt` in that thread first, or wait for it to finish.
thread_agent.idle_closed: 💤 Thread agent closed due to inactivity. Send a message to restart.
thread_agent.archive_deferred: ⚠️ Thread <#%s> was archived while its agent is still working. It will be closed after the current task finishes.
channel_agent.idle_closed: 💤 Channel agent closed due to inactivity. Send a message to restart.
warn.attachments_large: ⚠️ Total attachment size ~%d MB may exceed processing limit (%d MB). Consider sending files in batches or adjusting MAX_SCANNER_BUFFER_MB.
error.agent_read:
⚠️ Agent communication lost: %v
Use /reset to restart.
cmd.memory.opt.value: Content to add, or index to remove
cmd.flashmemory.opt.value: Content to add, or index to remove
memory.empty: 📭 No memory rules set. Use `!memory add <rule>` to add one.
memory.parent_scope: ℹ️ Memory rules are scoped to the parent channel and apply to its threads.
flashmemory.empty: 📭 No flash memory set. Use `!flashmemory add <rule>` to add one.
flashmemory.parent_scope: ℹ️ Flash memory is scoped to the parent channel session and applies to its threads.
mcp.catalog.empty: No MCP servers discovered. Configure `KIRO_MCP_CONFIG` or `~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json`.
mcp.catalog.footer: Catalog servers are disabled by default. When enabled, they run through the bot MCP policy proxy so channel policy can filter tools before the agent sees or calls them.
mcp.checklist.footer: Unchecked servers are in the catalog but disabled for this channel. Use `/mcp manage` to enable servers, scan tools, and manage tool-level access.
mcp.status.requires_server: MCP status requires a server name.
mcp.status.format:
**MCP policy**
- server: `%s` (%s, catalog=%s)
- channel: `%s`
- policy: `%s`
- allow all tools: `%s`
- tools: `%s`
doctor.acp_tool_policy.trust_all: ⚠️ ACP tool policy: trust all tools
doctor.acp_tool_policy.deny_default: ✅ ACP tool policy: deny by default
doctor.env.desc.KIRO_API_KEY: headless auth key (alternative to kiro-cli login)
doctor.env.desc.KIRO_MCP_CONFIG: MCP catalog source; agents use bot-managed runtime config
doctor.env.desc.CHANNEL_AGENT_IDLE_SEC: channel agent idle timeout seconds [default: 0 = never]
doctor.env.desc.PREFLIGHT_MODE: preflight mode: warn or enforce [default: warn]
doctor.env.desc.CRON_TIMEZONE: cron timezone [default: service process local timezone]
doctor.env.desc.USAGE_TIMEZONE: usage report timezone [default: CRON_TIMEZONE, then service process local timezone]
agent.no_session: No active agent. Send a message first to start one.
cwd.setup.next_steps: Next step: review MCP tool access or create the agent context file.
cmd.steering.desc: Manage this channel's agent context file
cmd.steering.sub.status: Show the agent context file status
cmd.steering.sub.create: Create the agent context file if missing
steering.forbidden: ❌ You need Discord channel management permissions to manage agent context.
steering.usage: Usage: `!steering status`. Use `/steering create` to fill in reusable agent context, or `/steering edit` to edit with a private modal.
steering.create.slash_only: Use `/steering create` or the setup button to fill in reusable agent context before creating the steering file.
steering.edit.slash_only: Use `/steering edit` to open the private editor.
steering.status.missing:
Agent context file is not created yet.
File: `%s`
Path: `%s`
Use `/steering create` to create it.
steering.exists:
Agent context file exists: `%s`
Path: `%s`
Size: `%d` bytes
steering.edit.too_large: ❌ `%s` is too large for Discord modal editing (%d bytes). Edit it directly in the project and use `/steering status` to verify.
steering.create.modal.title: Create Agent Context
steering.create.modal.background_ph: Project, channel, recurring task, users, or desired outcomes.
steering.create.modal.working_style_ph: Reply style, repeated workflow, quality bar, collaboration preferences.
steering.create.modal.references_ph: Commands, paths, docs, verification steps, or routine references.
steering.create.modal.constraints_ph: Do-not-touch files, forbidden output, secrets policy, approval rules.
steering.create.modal.extra_ph: Any reusable context that does not fit the other fields.
steering.modal.title: Edit Agent Context
